points.name: Basic Point A — Left
points.location: Approximately 1 cm lateral to the sagittal line of the face. A 2 cm vertical line that runs from 1 cm above the hairline to 1 cm below the hairline.
points.shortDescription: Neck and cranial problems including cervical vertebrae C1–C7, dental pain, eyes, ears, swallowing, and throat.
points.longDescription: Located approximately 1 cm lateral to the sagittal line of the face. A 2 cm vertical line runs from 1 cm above the hairline to 1 cm below the hairline. Treats neck problems affecting cervical vertebrae C1–C7 and cranial problems such as dental pain, eye conditions, ear problems, difficulties swallowing, and throat conditions.

points.name: Basic Point A — Right
points.location: Approximately 1 cm lateral to the sagittal line of the face. A 2 cm vertical line that runs from 1 cm above the hairline to 1 cm below the hairline.
points.shortDescription: Neck and cranial problems including cervical vertebrae C1–C7, dental pain, eyes, ears, swallowing, and throat.
points.longDescription: Located approximately 1 cm lateral to the sagittal line of the face. A 2 cm vertical line runs from 1 cm above the hairline to 1 cm below the hairline. Treats neck problems affecting cervical vertebrae C1–C7 and cranial problems such as dental pain, eye conditions, ear problems, difficulties swallowing, and throat conditions.

points.name: Basic Point B — Left
points.location: Approximately 2 cm lateral to the sagittal line. The point is on the natural hairline.
points.shortDescription: Neck, upper shoulder, shoulder blade, nape, sore throat, and thyroid gland problems.
points.longDescription: Located approximately 2 cm lateral to the sagittal line, on the natural hairline. Addresses problems of the neck, upper shoulder, and shoulder blade (upper trapezius muscle), pain in the nape, sore throat, and conditions of the thyroid gland.

points.name: Basic Point B — Right
points.location: Approximately 2 cm lateral to the sagittal line. The point is on the natural hairline.
points.shortDescription: Neck, upper shoulder, shoulder blade, nape, sore throat, and thyroid gland problems.
points.longDescription: Located approximately 2 cm lateral to the sagittal line, on the natural hairline. Addresses problems of the neck, upper shoulder, and shoulder blade (upper trapezius muscle), pain in the nape, sore throat, and conditions of the thyroid gland.

points.name: Basic Point C — Left
points.location: The line begins 1 cm above the hairline and continues 1 cm below the hairline at an approximate angle of 45° to the nasal bridge. The mid-point of this 2 cm line is the GB-13 acupuncture point (on the superior border of the temporalis muscle).
points.shortDescription: Neurological problems between the shoulder and hand.
points.longDescription: A 2 cm line starting 1 cm above the hairline, running at approximately 45° to the nasal bridge. The midpoint corresponds to the GB-13 acupuncture point on the superior border of the temporalis muscle. Used for any neurological problems occurring between the shoulder and hand.

points.name: Basic Point C — Right
points.location: The line begins 1 cm above the hairline and continues 1 cm below the hairline at an approximate angle of 45° to the nasal bridge. The mid-point of this 2 cm line is the GB-13 acupuncture point (on the superior border of the temporalis muscle).
points.shortDescription: Neurological problems between the shoulder and hand.
points.longDescription: A 2 cm line starting 1 cm above the hairline, running at approximately 45° to the nasal bridge. The midpoint corresponds to the GB-13 acupuncture point on the superior border of the temporalis muscle. Used for any neurological problems occurring between the shoulder and hand.

points.name: Basic Point D — Left
points.location: An imaginary line that runs from the outer corner of the eye to the ear base and crosses the front edge of the sideburn (approximately 1 cm superior to the zygomatic bone). The extra D point is just below the ear base where the face meets the ear.
points.shortDescription: All problems of the diaphragm and below; pelvis, legs, lower back, gynecological, bowel and bladder problems.
points.longDescription: Located along an imaginary line from the outer corner of the eye to the ear base, crossing the front edge of the sideburn approximately 1 cm superior to the zygomatic bone. An extra D point sits just below the ear base where the face meets the ear. Addresses all problems of the diaphragm and below, including orthopedic and neurological conditions of the pelvis, legs, and lower back, gynecological problems, and bowel and bladder disorders.

points.name: Basic Point D — Right
points.location: An imaginary line that runs from the outer corner of the eye to the ear base and crosses the front edge of the sideburn (approximately 1 cm superior to the zygomatic bone). The extra D point is just below the ear base where the face meets the ear.
points.shortDescription: All problems of the diaphragm and below; pelvis, legs, lower back, gynecological, bowel and bladder problems.
points.longDescription: Located along an imaginary line from the outer corner of the eye to the ear base, crossing the front edge of the sideburn approximately 1 cm superior to the zygomatic bone. An extra D point sits just below the ear base where the face meets the ear. Addresses all problems of the diaphragm and below, including orthopedic and neurological conditions of the pelvis, legs, and lower back, gynecological problems, and bowel and bladder disorders.

points.name: Basic Point E — Left
points.location: An oblique 2 cm line that starts 1 cm lateral to the midline above the eyebrow.
points.shortDescription: Thoracic vertebrae, cardiovascular, respiratory, liver, stomach, and diaphragm conditions.
points.longDescription: An oblique 2 cm line starting 1 cm lateral to the midline above the eyebrow. Treats problems of the thoracic vertebrae, cardiovascular conditions, breathing difficulties and respiratory conditions, and disorders of the liver, stomach, and diaphragm.

points.name: Basic Point E — Right
points.location: An oblique 2 cm line that starts 1 cm lateral to the midline above the eyebrow.
points.shortDescription: Thoracic vertebrae, cardiovascular, respiratory, liver, stomach, and diaphragm conditions.
points.longDescription: An oblique 2 cm line starting 1 cm lateral to the midline above the eyebrow. Treats problems of the thoracic vertebrae, cardiovascular conditions, breathing difficulties and respiratory conditions, and disorders of the liver, stomach, and diaphragm.

points.name: Basic Point F — Left
points.location: The point is located on the striking out point of the mastoid bone (approx 1.5 cm above the mastoid process).
points.shortDescription: All types of sciatica and piriformis muscle problems.
points.longDescription: Located on the prominent point of the mastoid bone, approximately 1.5 cm above the mastoid process. Primarily used for all types of sciatica and problems of the piriformis muscle.

points.name: Basic Point F — Right
points.location: The point is located on the striking out point of the mastoid bone (approx 1.5 cm above the mastoid process).
points.shortDescription: All types of sciatica and piriformis muscle problems.
points.longDescription: Located on the prominent point of the mastoid bone, approximately 1.5 cm above the mastoid process. Primarily used for all types of sciatica and problems of the piriformis muscle.

points.name: Basic Point G — Left
points.location: In the arch about 3 mm above the basic D point on the Yin aspect; at the edge of the mastoid bone on the Yang aspect.
points.shortDescription: Knee problems — acute trauma (Yin) and chronic conditions (Yang).
points.longDescription: On the Yin aspect, located in the arch about 3 mm above the basic D point. On the Yang aspect, at the edge of the mastoid bone. Used for knee problems due to direct trauma (Yin aspect — acute), and for chronic knee conditions (Yang aspect).

points.name: Basic Point G — Right
points.location: In the arch about 3 mm above the basic D point on the Yin aspect; at the edge of the mastoid bone on the Yang aspect.
points.shortDescription: Knee problems — acute trauma (Yin) and chronic conditions (Yang).
points.longDescription: On the Yin aspect, located in the arch about 3 mm above the basic D point. On the Yang aspect, at the edge of the mastoid bone. Used for knee problems due to direct trauma (Yin aspect — acute), and for chronic knee conditions (Yang aspect).

points.name: Basic Point H — Left
points.location: Approximately 1 cm above the basic B point.
points.shortDescription: Lower back problems and knee problems.
points.longDescription: Located approximately 1 cm above the basic B point. Highly effective for lower back problems and is also a key point for knee conditions.

points.name: Basic Point H — Right
points.location: Approximately 1 cm above the basic B point.
points.shortDescription: Lower back problems and knee problems.
points.longDescription: Located approximately 1 cm above the basic B point. Highly effective for lower back problems and is also a key point for knee conditions.

points.name: Basic Point I — Yin
points.location: Approximately 2–3 cm anterior to the apex line and approximately 4 cm above the apex of the ear.
points.shortDescription: Augments Point D; sciatica, lumbago, lower extremity weakness and paralysis. The I point has developed into a whole-body somatotope.
points.longDescription: The Yin I point is located approximately 2–3 cm anterior to the apex line and 4 cm above the apex of the ear, in the temple region just above the ear. Originally used to augment the effect of Point D, it has become a significant point in its own right. In recent years the I somatotope has expanded to cover the whole body, and needling this point exerts an effect on the entire body.

points.name: Basic Point I — Yang
points.location: Approximately 2–3 cm posterior to the apex line and approximately 4 cm above the apex of the ear.
points.shortDescription: Augments Point D; sciatica, lumbago, lower extremity weakness and paralysis. The I point has developed into a whole-body somatotope.
points.longDescription: The Yang I point is located approximately 2–3 cm posterior to the apex line and 4 cm above the apex of the ear, in the temple region just above the ear. Originally used to augment the effect of Point D, it has become a significant point in its own right. In recent years the I somatotope has expanded to cover the whole body, and needling this point exerts an effect on the entire body.
